vitals.terrain: Brutal gradients and remote moorland roads

course_description.overview: The Struggle Moors is a challenging road cycling event in the North York Moors, starting from Sutton Bank National Park Centre, featuring the toughest cycling terrain with extreme hills and sustained climbs over approximately 112 km and ~3,000 m elevation.[1][3][4]

key_segments.name: Sutton Bank
key_segments.description: Iconic steep climb in the North York Moors National Park.

key_segments.name: Remote Moorland Roads
key_segments.description: Brutal gradients on isolated moorland routes.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Extreme terrain with frequent steep hills and long sustained climbs, part of the Struggle series complementing the Dales edition.[4]

logistics.getting_there: Start location at Sutton Bank National Park Centre, North Yorkshire; accessible by car or public transport to nearby areas like Harrogate.[1]
logistics.accommodation: Options in nearby North Yorkshire towns such as Harrogate or local National Park areas.[2]
logistics.registration_details: Registration opens January 1, 2026; entry from £49; currently sold out.[3][4]
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bike recommended for paved moorland roads and steep gradients.
logistics.local_considerations: Remote moorland; prepare for variable weather in July; Sutton Bank area known for steep terrain.

final_verdict.one_liner: Brutal road challenge through remote North York Moors with savage climbs.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong road cyclists seeking epic suffering on steep UK gradients.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for value and unique moorland adventure at low entry cost.
pro_tips: Train on 20%+ gradients like Sutton Bank.
Book early as it sells out.
Pack layers for changeable moorland weather.
Combine with Dales Struggle for series completion.
